title: Touch 3: Capability Deck Generation
description: Walk through the complete Touch 3 capability deck workflow: capability area selection, structure-driven assembly, and the full 3-gate HITL review with a lowfi refine demo.

steps.narration: Navigate to the Touch 3 Capability Alignment page for the Meridian Dynamics deal. This is where you build a detailed deck showcasing specific capability areas relevant to your prospect.
The landing state shows Meridian Dynamics ready for Touch 3 generation. From here, you can launch the capability deck workflow and refine the output at each review gate.

callout.text: Select areas relevant to this deal

steps.narration: Once generation starts, the workflow switches into an in-progress state while AtlusDeck assembles the capability deck and prepares the first review gate.
The skeleton gate shows the proposed deck structure organized by capability area. Each section has dedicated slides mapping to the selected areas: Cloud, Data, and Security.
Notice how the outline groups the story into clear capability sections for Cloud Infrastructure, Data Analytics, Security, and a closing summary section.
After approving the skeleton, the AI generates draft content for each capability slide. Review the content blocks to ensure they match Meridian's needs.
Zoom in on the Cloud Infrastructure slides. The draft content covers migration methodology, cost reduction metrics, and infrastructure automation capabilities.
When you request a lowfi refinement, the workflow returns to a short in-progress state while it sharpens the draft messaging before final production.
The refined content now includes Meridian-specific references, more precise technical details, and stronger value propositions for each capability area.
Compare the refined slides. Notice the personalized references to Meridian's AWS environment, client engagement data, and service boundaries throughout.
After approving the refined content, the final deck preview loads with Meridian-specific branding and an eight-slide structure ready for review.
The preview reinforces the capability themes with branded section accents: Cloud in blue, Data in purple, and Security in red.
The capability deck is now saved to Google Drive, with the eight-slide preview available on-page and ready to open for presentation.
